title: Add job deserialization from Redis queue
description: Implements job deserialization for the background worker, reading serialized Python objects from Redis.
diff:
diff --git a/workers/deserializer.py b/workers/deserializer.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c9d0e1f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/workers/deserializer.py
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+import pickle
+from redis import Redis
+
+def fetch_next_job(redis: Redis, queue: str) -> object:
+    raw = redis.lpop(queue)
+    if raw is None:
+        return None
+    # Deserialize the job object from the queue
+    return pickle.loads(raw)
comments: security-walt: CRITICAL: pickle.loads on untrusted data is a Remote Code Execution (RCE) vector. An attacker who can push data to this Redis queue can execute arbitrary Python code on the worker. Use JSON or msgpack for serialization instead.
xena: This is CVE-level severity. pickle.loads is equivalent to eval() on untrusted data.
author-yuri: The Redis queue is internal only.
security-walt: If any service that writes to this queue is compromised, the worker becomes an RCE target. Never use pickle for deserialization.
author: yuri
